title: fix(core): prevent supervisor messages from leaking into sub-agent thread

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: send correct threadId to subagent
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relationship: related — fixed a different sub-agent threading bug, merged the day after this PR opened

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: feat(core): supervisor pattern for multi-agent coordination
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relationship: parent feature — introduced the supervisor delegation code this PR fixes

summary: When a supervisor agent delegates to a sub-agent, the supervisor's full conversation history (filteredContextMessages) was included in the messages array passed to the sub-agent. Because MessageHistory persists all messages with source: 'input', this caused supervisor tool call records to be written into the sub-agent's thread, producing duplicate/nested badges in the studio UI. The fix moves filteredContextMessages from the messages array to the context option, which MessageList marks as source: 'context' and MessageHistory excludes from persistence. The sub-agent LLM still sees the full history, but it's no longer saved to the sub-agent's thread.
maintainerNotes: Well-written PR with clear root cause analysis. Two concerns to address before merging: (1) CodeRabbit flagged that the context param is only added to generate/resumeGenerate/generateLegacy paths but NOT to the stream/resumeStream paths (lines ~3082-3115). This means streaming sub-agent delegations will lose supervisor history entirely — a regression. The author needs to add `context: filteredContextMessages as any` to both stream() and resumeStream() calls. (2) The `as any` cast on filteredContextMessages bypasses type safety between MastraDBMessage[] and the expected context type — minor but worth noting. (3) No automated tests are included; the test plan is manual-only. CI is stuck in PENDING state (core tests, memory tests, etc. haven't run — likely needs maintainer approval to trigger). Related PR #13868 (sub-agent threadId fix) was merged after this PR was opened, so verify there are no conflicts with that change.
